incidents.vector: Attackers compromised a third-party AI tool (Context.ai) via an infostealer on a vendor employee's device, then used stolen OAuth tokens to seize a Vercel employee's Google Workspace account and pivot into Vercel environments.
incidents.impact: A limited subset of Vercel customers had credentials and non-sensitive environment variables exposed; Mandiant and law enforcement are investigating, and attackers linked to the ShinyHunters brand demanded a $2 million ransom. Customers across the global developer ecosystem have been told to rotate every key.

incidents.vector: Attackers exploited a flaw in Kelp DAO's rsETH LayerZero cross-chain bridge to mint and withdraw restaked Ether tokens without backing collateral.
incidents.impact: Roughly $292-300 million in crypto assets were stolen, making it the largest crypto heist of 2026 so far; about $175 million has already been routed through THORChain, Umbra and BitTorrent to launder the proceeds.

incidents.vector: Attackers pre-positioned fake token pools for days, then exploited a vulnerability in the protocol's margin trading feature to drain funds through the rigged pools.
incidents.impact: $18.4 million in crypto assets stolen from the DeFi platform.

incidents.victim: Web3 developers and cryptocurrency holders worldwide
incidents.vector: DPRK operators posed as recruiters on LinkedIn using AI-generated fake companies (including a Mexico-registered shell), then tricked Web3 developers into running trojanized coding-assessment tools that deployed BeaverTail, OtterCookie and InvisibleFerret malware.
incidents.impact: Expel researchers documented theft of over $12 million in crypto from 26,584 wallets on 2,726 infected developer machines in the first three months of 2026, run by a 31-person DPRK team across six sub-teams; campaign continues as unemployed developers remain a prime target.

incidents.victim: Managed service providers, dental software vendor, and dozens of downstream customers
incidents.vector: Attackers mass-exploited unpatched BeyondTrust Bomgar Remote Support instances via CVE-2026-1731, a critical unauthenticated remote code execution flaw, then pushed secondary RMM tools (AnyDesk, Atera) onto domain controllers for persistence.
incidents.impact: Huntress observed five confirmed intrusions in two weeks, including an April 15 MSP breach that forced mass isolation of 78 businesses with follow-on compromise of four downstream customers, and multiple LockBit 3.0 ransomware deployments.

incidents.victim: LiteLLM, Checkmarx, Trivy (security and AI dev tools) and downstream users
incidents.vector: Group known as TeamPCP used stolen developer credentials (likely sourced from infostealer logs) to push credential-harvesting payloads into LiteLLM (≈97M monthly downloads), Checkmarx GitHub Actions, and Aqua Security's Trivy, chaining compromises across five software distribution ecosystems in five days.
incidents.impact: Malware silently exfiltrated access keys, cloud credentials, secrets and AI API keys from every organization installing the compromised packages; TeamPCP claims to hold 300+ GB of stolen data and is extorting victims, with spillover expected to fuel payroll-redirect and logistics fraud campaigns.

incidents.victim: Ukrainian prosecutors and militaries of Greece, Romania, and Serbia
incidents.vector: Spear-phishing and credential theft against government email accounts, attributed to a GRU-linked cyber unit.
incidents.impact: Attackers compromised email accounts of more than 170 Ukrainian prosecutors to harvest investigative material, with parallel intrusions into NATO-allied militaries in Greece, Romania and Serbia.

incidents.victim: Government agencies and enterprises using SOHO routers worldwide
incidents.vector: Russian GRU operators exploited known vulnerabilities in end-of-life Mikrotik and TP-Link small-office routers to reconfigure DNS settings, redirecting users through attacker-controlled DNS servers to silently intercept Microsoft Office OAuth authentication tokens without deploying any malware.
incidents.impact: At its December 2025 peak, the campaign ensnared more than 18,000 routers across 200+ organizations and 5,000 consumer devices, including foreign ministries, law enforcement and email providers; Microsoft, Lumen's Black Lotus Labs, and the UK NCSC published concurrent advisories.

incidents.vector: Unspecified cyberattack prompted the city to take its IT network offline; surrounding Leon County severed links to prevent contamination, a pattern typical of ransomware incidents.
incidents.impact: Multiple Tallahassee city websites and public services remain unavailable, and the county has disconnected from city systems as a precaution.

incidents.vector: Attackers breached the City Attorney's Office; initial access vector has not been disclosed publicly.
incidents.impact: 7.7 TB of sensitive material was published online, including LAPD officer personal data, witness names, medical records, case files and internal affairs investigations.

incidents.vector: Attack attributed to a group calling itself FlamingChina that first emerged in February 2026; exact intrusion method undisclosed.
incidents.impact: Petabytes of data, including files marked 'secret' and containing Chinese military and government material, were leaked online; investigators took weeks to verify authenticity due to the sheer volume of data.

incidents.vector: Attackers accessed the company's crypto wallets in March; specific intrusion method has not been disclosed publicly.
incidents.impact: Roughly $3.6 million in cryptocurrency stolen from the operator of more than 25,000 Bitcoin ATMs worldwide; incident disclosed in an SEC 8-K filing.
